nodes.context: Hong Kong-based triad with an American branch operating in San Francisco.
San Francisco Chinatown street gang, part of the Hop Sing Tong.
Chinese fraternal association based in San Francisco, also known as Chinese Freemasons.
San Francisco Chinatown gang, rival to Hop Sing and Wo Hop To.
Chinese fraternal association in San Francisco, parent organization of Hop Sing Boys.
Italian-American organized crime syndicate mentioned in passing.
A Chinese fraternal association (tong) based in San Francisco, involved in organized crime.
A Chinese fraternal association based in San Francisco, led by Raymond Chow after 2006.
A Hong Kong-based triad organized crime syndicate with an American branch.
A street gang associated with San Francisco Chinatown.
Italian-American organized crime syndicate; an undercover FBI agent posed as an emissary from it.
Hong Kong-based triad with an American branch.
Chinese fraternal association based in San Francisco, California.

edges.evidence_quote: Under the alliance, the Hop Sing and Wo Hop To merged, and an umbrella organization, Tien Hu Wui, was formed to oversee their combined business.
edges.context: The Hop Sing and Wo Hop To merged under an alliance led by Peter Chong and Raymond Chow.

edges.evidence_quote: At about the same time, the Wah Ching and Wo Hop To were struggling for power in San Francisco.
edges.context: The two groups were in a violent power struggle in San Francisco.

edges.evidence_quote: In the wake of the 1977 shooting, the Wah Ching were ascendant and the Hop Sing were chased out of San Francisco.
edges.context: After the Golden Dragon massacre, Wah Ching drove the Hop Sing out of San Francisco.

edges.evidence_quote: Leung was also the dragon head of the Hung Moon Ghee Kong Tong (CKT), a fraternal association in San Francisco sometimes referred to as the Chinese Freemasons.
edges.context: Allen Leung was a leader of both the Hop Sing Tong and the Ghee Kung Tong, indicating a structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: Allen Leung was president of the Hop Sing Tong (HST) and dragon head of the CKT
edges.context: Allen Leung held leadership roles in both organizations simultaneously, indicating a structural tie.

edges.evidence_quote: Chow joined the CKT at around this time. According to Kongphet "Fat Joe" Chanthavong, Chow convened a meeting in mid-2005 in the back room of an Oakland bar, instructing Chanthavong and two others to murder Leung. At the time, Allen Leung was president of the Hop Sing Tong (HST) and dragon head of the CKT; because the HST had refused to loan money to Chow to start a business, he had become upset and told his associate, Wen Bing "Skinny Ray" Lei, that Lei should replace Leung as head of the HST.
edges.context: Chow ordered the murder of the Hop Sing Tong president after the tong refused him a loan.

edges.evidence_quote: Chow was secretly recorded by the FBI while he was conferring with "George" Heng Nieh (Chow's driver), stating that he did not want the CKT involved with "Jordan", the undercover agent posing as a member of the Mafia, for fear the CKT would be labeled 'an underworld society' that participated in organized crime.
edges.context: Chow avoided involvement with the undercover agent posing as Mafia, indicating no cooperation.

edges.evidence_quote: ties to a San Francisco Chinatown street gang and an organized crime syndicate, including the American branch of the Hong Kong -based triad Wo Hop To and the Hop Sing Boys.
edges.context: The text mentions both organizations as part of the criminal ties of the subject, but does not assert a direct relationship between them.
